launcher.title: The full suite
launcher.blurb: 29 analytical dashboards over live Pentaho Data Catalog metadata — one launcher.

groups.name: Executive

items.title: Unified Executive Scorecard
items.blurb: Every domain's headline KPI on one board, with trend sparklines and drill-through into each dashboard.

items.title: Executive Scorecard
items.blurb: The same board built natively on the Pentaho CDF framework (CCC charts) — toggle Custom ⇄ Framework in the header.

groups.value: Answer “is our data estate healthy?” at a glance — one board for leadership spanning coverage, risk and reliability.

groups.name: Observability

items.title: Estate Observability Command Center
items.blurb: Single pane of glass: catalog health, governance, sensitivity, freshness, completeness — every panel drills into its domain.

items.title: Observability Command Center
items.blurb: The single-pane-of-glass rebuilt natively on Pentaho CDF — KPIs, CCC charts, and a source scorecard.

items.title: Catalog Observability
items.blurb: True Pentaho CDF dashboard — CCC charts and CDF filters over CDA.

items.title: Data Freshness & Staleness
items.blurb: CDF twin — scan-freshness pie, modified-age and stale-by-source bars.

items.title: Catalog Growth & Discovery
items.blurb: CDF twin — cumulative growth line, assets-by-source pie, per-month discovery bars, and recently-discovered assets.

items.title: Application & Access Reach
items.blurb: CDF twin — which applications touch the catalog: top apps by access, type and source breakdowns, and a reach-detail table across 207 access events / 46.6 TB.

items.title: Catalog Adoption
items.blurb: CDF twin — how actively the catalog is classified, governed and used: adoption coverage, top apps & policies, and classified assets by source.

items.title: Pipeline & Job Observability
items.blurb: CDF twin — DataOps reliability: 8.2K runs at 92% success, success & volume trends, event lifecycle, by-integration health, and the top failing jobs.

groups.value: Spot problems before users do — monitor catalog health, governance, freshness and pipeline reliability across every source.

groups.name: Governance & Privacy

items.title: Sensitive Data Domains
items.blurb: Governance risk map: which business domains carry HIGH/MEDIUM sensitive data, as a domain × sensitivity heatmap.

items.title: Sensitive Data Domains
items.blurb: CDF twin rendering the cross-tab as a stacked CCC bar — same data, native framework.

items.title: Policy & Governance Coverage
items.blurb: Governed vs ungoverned assets, policy assignments, and coverage trended over time.

items.title: Policy & Governance Coverage
items.blurb: CDF twin — coverage pie, governed-by-source bars, policy types, and a 2-series coverage-over-time line.

items.title: Sensitive Data & Privacy
items.blurb: CDF twin — PII/classification reach, sensitivity scale, exposure by source, and a privacy term × source stacked bar over 625 classified assets.

items.title: Sensitive Data & Compliance Radar
items.blurb: An animated Sankey of sensitive data flow — which sensitivity levels (Internal/Confidential/Restricted) move into governed vs ungoverned destinations — plus sensitivity mix, bytes by sensitivity, restricted-flow risk, and a cross-boundary movement trend.

items.title: Sensitive Data & Compliance Radar
items.blurb: CDF twin — source-sensitivity→destination-governance stacked bar, sensitivity mix, bytes by sensitivity, restricted-flow risk, and a 2-series cross-boundary movement trend over 7.4K flows.

groups.value: Prove control of sensitive data — see what’s governed, what’s exposed, and where sensitivity concentrates, so policy gaps are visible, not guessed.

groups.name: Data Quality & Structure

items.title: Data Quality & Metadata Completeness
items.blurb: Completeness distribution, most-missing attributes, and an actionable lowest-completeness remediation list.

items.title: Data Quality & Metadata Completeness
items.blurb: CDF twin — completeness & profiling pies, most-missing attributes, and a remediation table.

items.title: Column Health & Key Discovery
items.blurb: Key-candidate detection (cardinality ≈ rows), dead/constant columns, and type mix across 10K+ columns.

items.title: Column Health & Key Discovery
items.blurb: CDF twin — health-mix pie, type bars, and key/dead-column tables on the framework.

items.title: Column Profiling & Statistics
items.blurb: CDF twin — null-% and uniqueness distribution pies, by-source bars, and highest-cardinality columns over 2.9K profiled columns.

items.title: Schema & Structure Explorer
items.blurb: CDF twin — catalog composition, column data types, key coverage, and the largest schemas across 754 tables / 11.5K columns.

items.title: Document & Unstructured Insights
items.blurb: CDF twin — files by extension, file assets by source, scan-volume trend, and an extension-detail table across 3.1K file assets.

groups.value: Trust the numbers — surface incomplete metadata, unprofiled assets and structural gaps so teams fix the worst offenders first.

groups.name: Storage, Cost & Lineage

items.title: Storage Footprint & Capacity
items.blurb: TB by source and type, top-heavy objects, and a cumulative storage-growth trend.

items.title: Storage Footprint & Capacity
items.blurb: CDF twin — TB by source, structured/unstructured, size distribution, and cumulative growth.

items.title: Cost Optimization & Sustainability
items.blurb: Monthly cost by source and sensitivity, reclaimable spend, and CO₂e footprint.

items.title: Cost Optimization & Sustainability
items.blurb: CDF twin — cost by source & sensitivity, reclaimable spend, and CO2e.

items.title: Redundancy & Duplicate Data
items.blurb: CDF twin — 233 TB reclaimable storage / $27.5K-a-month: reclaimable by source & sensitivity, duplicate assets, and top duplicate objects.

items.title: Data Movement Observability
items.blurb: Lineage throughput and pipeline reliability (16.7 TB moved) on the CDF framework.

items.title: Data Movement Flows (Sankey)
items.blurb: An animated Sankey + radial network topology of cross-platform data movement — 9.3 TB flowing source→destination (S3, Snowflake, Kafka, Postgres, BigQuery…), ribbons sized by volume; hover a platform to light its blast radius. Click a flow to drill into integration health.

items.title: Data Movement Flows
items.blurb: CDF twin — the same cross-platform flows as a CCC stacked bar (destination stacked by source), outbound-by-source, by-integration, and a top-flows table.

items.title: Data Integration Health
items.blurb: Platform-movement matrix, throughput trend, success rate, and top cross-platform flows.

items.title: Data Integration Health
items.blurb: Platform-movement stacked bar, throughput trend, success rate, and top cross-platform flows on the CDF framework.

groups.value: Cut spend and trace movement — find reclaimable storage and duplicates, attribute cost and carbon to sources, and follow data across platforms.

groups.name: Glossary & Stewardship

items.title: Glossary Hierarchy & Term Stewardship
items.blurb: Term adoption gap (576 defined vs in-use), hierarchy, types, and reach across 23 business glossaries.

items.title: Glossary Hierarchy & Term Stewardship
items.blurb: CDF twin — adoption pie, glossary/type/depth bars and reach on the framework.

items.title: Ownership & Stewardship Gaps
items.blurb: CDF twin — owner-coverage pie, unowned backlog by source, governance coverage, and a per-source stewardship scorecard across 14K assets.

items.title: Business Glossary & Term Reach
items.blurb: CDF twin — term reach by glossary, classification coverage, terms-per-glossary, and top terms across 161 in-use terms / 12 business glossaries.

groups.value: Make data findable and owned — measure business-glossary coverage and close the ownership gaps that stall self-service.
